name: Automate SMS and RCS marketing campaigns from Google Sheets with RCSZilla

parameters.content:
## SMS/RCS marketing automation

Run consent-based SMS or RCS-ready marketing campaigns from Google Sheets with n8n and RCSZilla.

This workflow scans a campaign queue, checks consent and opt-out fields, personalizes each message, spaces sends over time, queues messages through RCSZilla, and logs every queued or skipped contact.

Use SMS by default. Set the channel to RCS only when your RCSZilla account and sending route support it.

## Setup guide

1. Install community node: n8n-nodes-rcszilla.
2. Create Google Sheets and RCSZilla credentials.
3. Create a Google Sheet with tabs named Campaign Queue, Send Log, and Opt Outs.
4. Replace PASTE_SMS_RCS_MARKETING_SHEET_ID in all Google Sheets nodes. You can download a template here : https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11aQpfGRjxswKMASmEUsY9PBKHsonjuuTLSnbGJSm55Q/edit?usp=sharing
5. Connect your Android device in RCSZilla. Docs: https://docs.rcszilla.com/?page=get_started
6. Add one internal test row with consent = yes and status = ready.
7. Run manually first, inspect Send Log, then activate the schedule.

Only message contacts who gave marketing consent.

## Campaign Queue columns

Recommended columns:

message_id, phone, first_name, last_name, consent, opt_out, channel, segment, campaign_name, message_template, offer_text, offer_url, send_after, status

Required:

message_id: unique row ID
phone: recipient number with country code
consent: yes
status: ready

Optional:

channel: sms or rcs. Leave blank for sms.
send_after: future date/time before the row is eligible.
message_template supports {{first_name}}, {{offer_text}}, {{offer_url}}, {{campaign_name}}, and {{segment}}.

## Consent and opt-out

This workflow is for permission-based marketing.

It skips contacts without consent, skips opt-outs, deduplicates phone numbers per run, limits send volume, adds Reply STOP to opt out, and logs skipped rows.

Do not use purchased lists or unsolicited contacts. Keep consent records in your CRM, store, form, or Google Sheet.

## Opt-out webhook

Use this branch for inbound STOP replies from RCSZilla or another SMS/RCS provider.

Example payload:

{
  "from": "+40700000000",
  "message": "STOP",
  "source": "RCSZilla"
}

STOP, UNSUBSCRIBE, CANCEL, END, and QUIT are saved to the Opt Outs tab.

Use that tab to update your master contact list before the next campaign.

## 1. Check queue

Runs every 15 minutes and looks for rows where status is ready.

Keep the workflow inactive while testing. Use one internal number first.

## 2. Read campaign rows

Reads the Campaign Queue tab from Google Sheets.

The sheet acts as a lightweight campaign manager for message copy, audience segment, channel, timing, and row status.

## 3. Prepare messages

Validates each row, renders variables in the template, appends Reply STOP when needed, applies quiet hours, deduplicates numbers, and spaces eligible sends.

## 4. Send or skip

Eligible rows go to RCSZilla.

Rows with missing consent, opt-out flags, duplicate phones, future send_after dates, or non-ready status are logged as skipped.

## 5. Queue with RCSZilla

Queues each eligible SMS/RCS marketing message with RCSZilla.

The channel comes from the sheet. Keep it as sms unless your account supports another route.

## 6. Campaign logging

Queued contacts are marked as queued in the Campaign Queue tab and appended to Send Log.

Skipped contacts are also appended to Send Log with a reason, so you can clean the sheet before the next run.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { success: true, status: 'opt_out_saved', phone: $('Normalize opt-out reply').item.json.phone } }}
={{ { success: true, status: 'ignored', reason: 'No opt-out keyword or phone number found' } }}
